app.name: Bida
app.description: Household budgeting in one place. Income, costs, and what's left to spend.

datePicker.hint: Type a date or pick a suggestion

consent.title: Your Data, Your Privacy
consent.body: Everything you enter stays with you. We never sell your data or share it with anyone. You can delete all your data from Settings at any time.
consent.checkbox: I understand and agree to proceed

type.title: Who are we budgeting for?
type.helper: This helps us set up income and cost fields for your whole household.

partnerName.title: What should we call your partner?
partnerName.helper: Used only to personalise labels like 'Sarah's income'. Nothing else.
partnerName.label: Partner's name

children.title: Do you have any children?
children.helper: This unlocks a dedicated section for children's costs.

numChildren.title: How many children do you have?
numChildren.helper: Each child gets their own cost section later in the setup.

childDetails.title: Child {{n}} — a couple of quick details
childDetails.helper: The name is just for display. The age group shows the right cost fields.

s2.heading: Where you live and what you do
s2.body: Shapes what you pay. Let's get that right.

location.title: Where are you living?
location.helper: We use this to pre-fill local costs and show relevant financial context.

occupation.title: What best describes your work situation?
occupation.helper: This affects your income section and some cost fields — for example, health insurance.
occupation.partnerHelper: Affects income fields and insurance context.

s3.heading: Now the numbers
s3.body: Enter net amounts — what actually lands in your account. This is the number everything else is measured against.

q5.helperSelfEmployed: After business expenses and tax — your take-home average.
q5.helperStudent: Your regular stipend or scholarship amount.
q5.helperNotWorking: We'll skip income — you can add other sources below if you have any.
q5.helperOther: Your best estimate of average monthly take-home.
q5.notWorkingNote: No income to enter — you can add other sources below.

q5b.title: Any other sources of income?
q5b.helper: Rental income, dividends, financial support — anything regular.
q5b.helperRequired: Neither you nor your partner has employment income recorded. Add at least one regular income source so we can build your budget.
q5b.helperRequiredSolo: You don't have employment income recorded. Add at least one regular income source so we can build your budget.
q5b.requiredNote: At least one income source is required to continue.
q5b.validationNoIncome: We can't build your budget without income. Select Yes and enter at least one regular source — such as benefits, rental income, or financial support — or go back and enter {{name}}'s salary or your own income.
q5b.validationNoIncomeSolo: We can't build your budget without income. Select Yes and enter at least one regular source — such as benefits, rental income, or financial support.
q5b.validationOtherAmount: Please enter the amount for at least one income source.

q5c.title: Do you have savings?
q5c.helper: Your balance shows how many months of expenses you have covered.

q5d.title: What is your financial goal?
q5d.helper: Choose what you want Bida to help you focus on.
q5d.typeReduceCostsDesc: Cut spending and find savings in your budget
q5d.typeSaveMoneyDesc: Build savings toward a target or each month
q5d.typeReduceAndSaveDesc: Spend less and put the difference aside
q5d.saveModeTitle: How do you want to save?
q5d.saveModeHelper: Choose whether you are saving toward a specific target or an ongoing monthly amount.
q5d.saveModeTargetDesc: e.g. 250,000 Kč for a car by a target date
q5d.saveModeOngoingDesc: Set aside a fixed amount each month with no end date
q5d.targetHelper: Enter what you are saving for, how much you need, and when you want to reach it.
q5d.ongoingHelper: How much do you want to set aside each month?
q5d.validationSaveMode: Please choose how you want to save
q5d.validationOngoingAmount: Please enter your monthly savings target

s4.heading: Housing
s4.body: Housing is usually the biggest monthly expense. Let's get a clear picture.

q6.title: Do you rent, own, or live with family?
q6.helper: Choose what best describes your current situation.

q6a.title: How much is your monthly rent?
q6a.helper: If utilities are included in your rent and you know the amount, enter base rent only — you'll enter utilities separately next. If you don't know the split (dorm, all-inclusive, etc.), just enter the total and skip utilities.

q6b.title: How much do you pay for utilities?
q6b.helper: Gas, electricity, water — combined. An estimate is fine. Skip if you don't know or prefer not to separate.
q6b.skip: Skip — I don't know or it's included
q6b.modeLabel: How would you like to enter utilities?

q6c.title: Do you pay for internet separately?
q6c.helper: Skip if internet is included in your rent or utilities.

q6d.title: Do you have a mortgage?
q6d.helper: Select 'No' if you own the property outright.

q6e.title: How much is your monthly mortgage payment?
q6e.helper: Total monthly amount including principal and interest.

q6f.title: Any other regular home ownership costs?
q6f.helper: Maintenance fund, building insurance, service charge, property tax — anything recurring.

q6h.title: Do you contribute to household costs?
q6h.helper: Contributing to rent, groceries, bills — add any regular support.

q6g.title: Government and city charges
q6g.helper: Most households pay fixed statutory charges. We've pre-filled the most common ones — confirm or adjust.
q6g.wasteTaxNoteEstimated: {{amount}}/yr — estimated for {{summary}}. Municipal rules vary; edit if your charge differs.
q6g.dontPayTvLicence: I don't pay TV licence fees
q6g.dontPayRadioLicence: I don't pay Radio licence fees

s5.heading: Transport
s5.body: Cars, fuel, parking, public transport — let's see what you spend on getting around.

q7.title: Do you have a car or other vehicle?
q7.helper: Motorcycle, scooter, van — anything with an engine that you use regularly.

q7Count.title: How many vehicles do you have?
q7Count.helper: Tell us how many vehicles you own across each category.
q7Count.validation: Please add at least one vehicle

q7a.title: Fuel and running costs
q7a.helper: What type of fuel does your vehicle use, and roughly how much do you spend per month?
q7a.validation: Please enter your monthly fuel cost

q7b.title: Vehicle insurance
q7b.helper: Enter your insurance details — skip if you don't have separate vehicle insurance.

q7c.title: Zone parking or resident permits
q7c.helper: If you pay for zone parking or a resident permit in your city, enter the cost here.

q7d.title: MOT / STK and maintenance
q7d.helper: When does your MOT/STK expire? Add any planned maintenance items you're saving for.

q7bicycle.title: Bicycle maintenance
q7bicycle.helper: Add any planned bicycle maintenance or repairs you're saving for — like a new chain, brake pads, or a flat tyre fix.

q7e.title: Public transport
q7e.helper: Enter your regular public transport costs — skip if you don't use public transport.
q7e.skip: Skip — I don't use public transport
q7e.validation: Please enter the public transport cost

s6.heading: Health Insurance
s6.body: Health insurance varies by situation. Let's make sure everyone in your household is covered.

health.title: Health insurance for {{name}}
health.helper: Confirm how {{name}}'s health insurance is handled.
health.coveredByEmployerNote: In the Czech Republic, employers pay health insurance for their employees. No action needed.
health.renewalPlanLabel: When this contract ends, what do you plan to do?
health.renewalRenewHelper: Same coverage, same contract period, and the same total premium — we'll help you save for that payment.
health.premiumPaidInFullLabel: Was this contract period paid in full upfront?
health.premiumPaidInFullHelper: If you paid the full premium before the start date, we won't deduct it monthly now — we'll help you save for renewal instead.
health.renewReserveSummary: Save {{amount}} per month for {{months}} months to cover {{total}} at renewal.
health.renewReserveExplain: Your current period is already paid. This monthly reserve builds up toward the next payment.
health.renewReserveTightWarning: Renewal is only {{months}} months away and you may not have enough saved yet (about {{shortfall}} still needed). You can use a lower monthly amount if the suggested one feels too high.
health.budgetForRenewLabel: Include this monthly reserve in your budget?
health.budgetForRenewHelper: We'll deduct this from your daily spending allowance until the contract ends.
health.renewUseCustomHelper: Choose this if the suggested amount is too high for your budget right now.
health.renewalSwitchHelper: Different coverage, payment frequency, or price when this contract ends — enter your best estimate.
health.renewalEndHelper: You don't plan to continue this insurance after it expires.
health.switchPlanIntro: Estimate what you'll pay for the new policy. Prices can go up or down before you sign — you can update this later.
health.switchPremiumHelper: Total amount you expect to pay when the new policy starts (for the period shown below).
health.switchPremiumFrequencyHelper: How you expect to pay after the new policy starts. We spread the amount above over the months until your current contract ends.
health.validation: Please confirm this member's insurance details

q8.title: Health insurance for {{name}}
q8.helper: Confirm how {{name}}'s health insurance is handled.
q8.coveredByEmployerNote: In the Czech Republic, employers pay health insurance for their employees. No action needed.
q8.validation: Please confirm this member's insurance details

s7.heading: Children's Costs
s7.body: Let's track the regular costs for each child — from nursery to school supplies.

q9.title: Costs for {{name}}
q9.helper: These fields are based on {{name}}'s age group ({{ageGroup}}).

field.diapersHelper: Monthly cost of diapers, wipes, creams
field.formulaHelper: Monthly cost of formula and baby food
field.clothingHelper: Monthly average for clothing and shoes
field.toysHelper: Monthly average for toys, playgroups, activities
field.schoolSuppliesHelper: Monthly average for school supplies and materials
field.allowanceHelper: Monthly allowance given to the child
field.transportHelper: Monthly school transport or bus pass costs
field.extracurricularHelper: Sports, music lessons, tutoring — monthly average
field.savingsHelper: Monthly savings set aside for the child

s8.heading: Pets
s8.body: Our furry (and not-so-furry) friends come with costs. Let's track them.

q10.title: Do you have any pets?
q10.helper: Dogs, cats, birds, fish, rabbits — any animal you care for regularly.

q10a.heading: Your pets
q10a.title: Pet {{n}}
q10a.dogTaxHelper: In the Czech Republic, dog owners pay an annual dog tax. Pre-filled at 1 500 Kč.

s9.heading: Subscriptions
s9.body: Streaming, cloud storage, fitness apps — the small monthly costs that add up.

q11.title: What subscriptions do you have?
q11.helper: Tap to add common subscriptions, or enter custom ones below.
q11.validation: Please enter the cost for each subscription
q11.streamingFlag: You have {{count}} streaming subscriptions costing {{total}}/month total.

s10.heading: Other Regular Costs
s10.body: The rest of your regular outgoings — groceries, mobile, insurance, and more.

q12.title: What other regular costs do you have?
q12.helper: Tap to add common costs, or enter custom ones below.
q12.validation: Please enter the amount for each cost
q12.osvcNote: As a self-employed person (OSVČ), don't forget to include your social and health insurance contributions.

s11.heading: Debts & Loans
s11.body: Understanding your debts is the first step to managing them. Let's get a clear picture.

q13.title: Do you have any debts or loans?
q13.helper: Credit cards, personal loans, student loans, BNPL — anything you're currently paying off.

q13a.title: Your debts
q13a.helper: Enter the details of this debt.
q13a.minPaymentHelper: The minimum amount you need to pay each month to stay in good standing.
q13a.aprHelper: Annual Percentage Rate — the yearly interest rate on this debt.
q13a.highAprWarning: ⚠️ This APR is above 20%. Consider prioritising this debt.

s12.heading: Set your flexible spending budget
s12.body: Now let's set your flexible spending budget and decide how unspent money rolls over.

q14.title: Your flexible spending budget
q14.helper: This is what's left after fixed costs, debt payments, and savings. You can adjust it if needed.
q14.validation: Please enter your flexible budget amount
q14.displayFrequencyLabel: How you want to see your budget
q14.displayHelper: Summary stays monthly; preview uses your chosen period.

splitSlider.label: Adjust spending vs savings
splitSlider.helper: Slide left to lower your budget and send more to savings each month. Slide right to spend more.

q14.loadError: We could not load your household data. Check your connection and try again.

deductSavingsGoal.label: Reserve savings goal from spending budget?
deductSavingsGoal.helper: When enabled, your spending preview subtracts the monthly amount needed to reach your savings goal.
deductSavingsGoal.previewNote: {{deduction}} reserved for your goal · {{amount}} to spend

q14a.title: What happens to unspent budget?
q14a.helper: Choose how unspent money from your flexible budget carries over to the next month.
q14a.freeDesc: Any money you don't spend is added to next month's flexible budget — your daily allowance goes up until you use it.
q14a.cappedDesc: Unspent budget carries over, but only up to a limit you set — so savings don't pile up without bound.
q14a.resetDesc: Each month starts fresh. Unspent budget does not roll into next month's spending allowance.
q14a.capTypeMultiplierHelper: e.g. ×2 means rollover can reach twice your monthly flexible budget.
q14a.capTypeAmountHelper: Set a maximum rollover balance in currency — e.g. 5 000 Kč.
q14a.resetDestinationHelper: At month end, leftover flexible budget is routed to a tracked balance — so your numbers still add up.
q14a.resetLooseMoneyHelper: Leftover is recorded as loose cash — not added to next month's spending allowance or formal savings.
q14a.resetForfeitHelper: Leftover is recorded as loose cash — not added to next month's spending allowance or formal savings.
q14a.resetToSavingsHelper: Leftover budget is moved into your savings balance at the end of each month.
q14a.resetToOtherGoalHelper: Track it separately — e.g. holiday fund, car repair, or a one-off purchase.
q14a.validationCapAmount: Please enter a custom rollover cap amount
q14a.validationOtherGoal: Please name the goal you are setting money aside for

s13.heading: Check everything looks right
s13.body: Let's review everything before we start. You can always come back and edit later.

q15.title: Review your information
q15.helper: Expand a section if you need to check details.
q15.cta: Looks good — take me to my dashboard

dashboard.title: Dashboard

tabRoles.dashboard: Your spending budget today and what needs attention.
tabRoles.income: Household income and savings goals — edit amounts inline.
tabRoles.expenses: Fixed costs and debt payments already committed each month.
tabRoles.budget: How much is left for everyday spending after commitments.
tabRoles.summary: Full ledger with health metrics and section-by-section analysis.

ledgerStrip.toSpendNote: {{spend}} of {{available}} available set aside for everyday spending
ledgerStrip.a11y: Monthly plan: income, committed costs, available after commitments, spending budget

ledgerCascade.committedHint: Includes bills, subscriptions and debt payments
ledgerCascade.savedIncluded: Not separated from spending — enable deduct on Budget to protect this amount
ledgerCascade.costsReducedNone: No reduction yet — edit expenses to lower committed costs
ledgerCascade.toSpendIncludedGoal: Your savings goal pace is included in this amount
ledgerCascade.a11y: Monthly plan: income, committed costs, available, savings, spending budget, unallocated cash

saveFeedback.updated: Spending budget is now {{amount}} per month ({{delta}})
saveFeedback.unchanged: Saved. Spending budget unchanged at {{amount}} per month.

dashboard.tabComingSoon: This section is coming in a future update.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.body: We couldn't display the dashboard. Try again or refresh the app.

metricExplain.tapHint: Tap to see how this is calculated

flexibleBudget.title: Your spending budget
flexibleBudget.meaning: Money left for everyday spending after rent, bills, and debt payments. Toggle daily, weekly, or monthly to see it in the period that suits you.
flexibleBudget.meaningDeducted: Your spending budget after reserving the monthly amount needed for your savings goal. Toggle daily, weekly, or monthly to see it in the period that suits you.

fixedLoad.title: Fixed load
fixedLoad.meaning: This shows how much of your income is already spoken for by fixed costs and minimum debt payments. Above 80% feels tight; above 100% means commitments exceed income.

monthlyBuffer.title: Budget flexibility
monthlyBuffer.meaning: Cash left over after your spending budget is set aside. Think of it as wiggle room — positive means extra headroom; negative means the spending budget is higher than what you actually have left.
monthlyBuffer.formula: Income − fixed costs − debt payments − spending budget

recurringCosts.title: Recurring costs
recurringCosts.meaning: Subscriptions, insurance premiums, and debt minimum payments that repeat every month. These are the costs you can review when you want to free up money.

totalIncome.title: Total income
totalIncome.meaning: The sum of all household income converted to a monthly amount — salaries, benefits, and any other regular sources you entered during setup.
totalIncome.formula: Each income source converted to monthly, then added together

totalExpenses.title: Committed costs
totalExpenses.meaning: Fixed household costs plus minimum debt payments due each month. This is money already committed before everyday spending.

savingsGoalPlan.title: Saving goal
savingsGoalPlan.targetMeaning: The amount you want to save and the deadline you set during setup. We use this to work out how much to set aside each month.
savingsGoalPlan.ongoingMeaning: A fixed amount you plan to set aside every month with no end date — useful for building savings steadily over time.
savingsGoalPlan.noGoalMeaning: You have not set a savings goal yet. Add one in Income to see your target here.
savingsGoalPlan.reduceCostsMeaning: Your primary goal is to lower household spending rather than save toward a specific amount.

savingsGoal.title: Goal tracking
savingsGoal.meaning: Shows how much you have saved so far and how much to set aside per day, week, or month to reach your target by the deadline.
savingsGoal.ongoingMeaning: Shows your total saved balance and how much you plan to set aside on an ongoing basis. Toggle daily, weekly, or monthly to see the pace in the period that suits you.
savingsGoal.noGoalMeaning: Set a savings goal in Income to see how much you need to put aside and track your progress here.

hero.setGoalPrompt: Set a goal to track your monthly savings plan

home.spendingBudgetNoteDeducted: {{spend}} to spend · {{goal}} reserved for your goal

goalTracking.title: Goal tracking
goalTracking.ongoingLabel: Ongoing amount set aside each month
goalTracking.monthlyNeededLabel: Monthly amount needed to reach your goal
goalTracking.savePaceLabel: Save {{amount}} per {{period}} to reach your goal

home.monthlyNote: {{amount}} per month after fixed costs and debt payments
home.loadError: We could not load your household data.
home.emptySubtitle: Complete setup to see your household budget here.

health.fixedLoadLabel: Of income committed to fixed costs and debt
health.recurringLabel: Share of income for recurring bills

trackerSnapshot.title: Spending pace
trackerSnapshot.noLogs: No spending logged yet — your full allowance is available.
trackerSnapshot.a11y: Spending pace today and this week, open tracker

monthEndStatus.title: Month-end routing
monthEndStatus.a11y: Month-end routing status, open budget settings

recurring.title: Recurring commitments
recurring.subBudgetHeavy: Subscriptions use {{pct}}% of your flexible budget

kpi.committedCostsHint: Fixed costs + debt payments per month

chart.expensesSubtitle: Fixed costs by category — tap a row to see line items

headline.balanced: Your fixed costs and flexible budget look balanced.
headline.tight: Fixed costs use {{pct}}% of income — review your largest categories.
headline.overcommitted: Fixed costs and debt payments exceed {{pct}}% of income.
headline.topCategory: {{category}} is your largest cost at {{pct}}% of income.

housing.highShare: Housing takes {{pct}}% of income — worth reviewing if you can reduce it.

subscriptions.summary: {{count}} subscriptions cost {{pct}}% of income each month.
subscriptions.manyStreaming: {{count}} streaming services — you may be able to consolidate.
subscriptions.renewalsSoon: {{count}} renewals coming up in the next 30 days.

debts.summary: {{count}} debts with minimum payments each month.
debts.highApr: {{count}} high-interest debt(s) — extra payments will help most.

budget.summary: Flexible spending is {{pct}}% of income after fixed costs.
budget.negativeSurplus: Your flexible budget is higher than what's left after fixed costs.

goals.onTrack: Your goal looks achievable with your current flexible budget.
goals.atRisk: You may need to save more each month or adjust your goal date.

generic.summary: This category is {{pct}}% of your monthly income.

summaryScreen.intro: Full household ledger with insights on each section. Tap Edit to update any area.

expensesScreen.emptyPanel: No expenses recorded in this group yet.
expensesScreen.emptyHint: Tap Add below to record it here — no need to leave this screen.

helper.overview: All committed costs by category — rent, bills, debt payments, and more.
helper.fixed: Costs that stay roughly the same each month — rent, utilities, insurance, taxes.
helper.recurring: Regular but variable bills — subscriptions, transport, pets, and minimum debt payments.

expensesScreen.subtabEmpty: You don't have any {{type}} expenses yet.
expensesScreen.editSectionHint: This expense is part of a household section. Open the section editor to change details.

fixedRecurring.title: Fixed and recurring expenses
fixedRecurring.salaryImpact: {{amount}} of your {{income}} monthly income ({{pct}}%) goes to fixed and recurring expenses

goalsScreen.empty: You have not set a financial goal yet.
goalsScreen.reduceCostsHelper: Bida will focus on finding savings and trimming spending.
goalsScreen.ongoingHelper: A fixed amount you plan to set aside each month.
goalsScreen.onTrack: On track — you have {{amount}} flexible each month.
goalsScreen.gap: You need {{needed}} per month but only have {{available}} flexible.

savingsScreen.balanceHelper: Your current savings balance from onboarding.
savingsScreen.monthlyPlanHelper: From your budget split, savings goal, and rollover settings.
savingsScreen.noInflows: No monthly savings planned yet — adjust your budget or set a savings goal.
savingsScreen.resetToSavings: Leftover flexible budget is added to savings at month end.
savingsScreen.resetToOther: Leftover budget is set aside for: {{name}}
savingsScreen.resetLooseMoney: Leftover flexible budget is tracked as loose cash at month end.
savingsScreen.looseMoneyHelper: Unallocated money from months when you underspent your flexible budget.
savingsScreen.otherGoalHelper: Money set aside at month end for this goal.
savingsScreen.projectionHelper: Projected balance if you keep your planned monthly savings for the next year.
savingsScreen.projectionGoalHelper: Projection toward {{target}} by {{date}}, based on your planned monthly savings.

chart.empty: Add a savings balance or monthly savings plan to see a projection.

intro.title: Spending pace
intro.body: See how you're doing today, this week, and this month — plus where leftover budget will go at month end.
intro.loggingSoon: Expense logging is coming soon. Previews will update as you record spending.

trackerScreen.noSpendLogged: No spending logged yet — allowance shows your full budget for this period.

daily.title: Today
daily.helper: Your daily flexible allowance and spending so far.

status.under: Under today's pace — room to spend.

weekly.title: This week
weekly.helper: Monday–Sunday totals for your flexible budget.

status.under: Under this week's pace — you're ahead.

monthly.title: This month
monthly.helper: Month-end routing based on spending so far and your rollover settings.
monthly.goesTo: If you keep this pace, leftover goes to
monthly.noLeftover: No leftover projected — you've used your flexible budget (or more).

route.rolloverCappedSplit: {{rollover}} to rollover · {{loose}} to loose cash (over cap)

monthEndHistory.title: Past month-end routing
monthEndHistory.helper: How leftover flexible budget was routed when each month closed.

budgetScreen.spendingAfterDeduction: {{amount}} to spend after reserving {{deduction}} for your goal
budgetScreen.savingsShift: {{amount}} per month redirected to savings from your flexible budget

unallocatedSlider.title: Shift unallocated cash to savings
unallocatedSlider.helper: {{amount}} available after commitments. Slide to split between spending and savings.
unallocatedSlider.a11y: Adjust how unallocated cash is split between spending and savings

deductSavingsGoal.label: Reserve savings goal from spending budget
deductSavingsGoal.helper: Subtract the monthly savings needed for your goal from the spending budget shown on the dashboard.

alertsScreen.empty: No alerts right now — you're up to date.

types.negativeSurplus: Spending budget exceeds what's left by {{amount}}
types.overcommitted: Fixed costs and debt use {{pct}}% of income

incomeScreen.emptyPanel: No income recorded in this group yet.
incomeScreen.subtabEmpty: You don't have any {{type}} income yet.
incomeScreen.otherEmpty: You haven't added any other income sources yet.
incomeScreen.otherEmptyHint: Freelance work, benefits, rental income — add any regular source here.

sectionEdit.loadError: We could not load this section. Try again later.
sectionEdit.saveError: We could not save your changes. Try again.

income.helper: Update amounts directly — changes save to your household budget immediately.
income.saveMode: How do you want to save?

validation.saveMode: Please choose how you want to save
validation.ongoing: Please enter your monthly savings target

budget.helper: Set your flexible spending budget and how unspent money rolls over.
budget.displayFrequency: How you want to see your budget
budget.deductSavingsGoal: Reserve savings goal from spending budget?
budget.effectiveSpend: {{amount}} available to spend after goal reservation

debts.helper: Edit balances and minimum payments for each debt.
debts.empty: No debts recorded. Add one below if needed.
debts.validation: Each debt needs a balance and minimum payment

subscriptions.helper: Update subscription costs and billing frequency.
subscriptions.validation: Each subscription needs a cost amount

otherCosts.helper: Update other recurring costs and how often they are paid.

pets.helper: Update food and vet costs for each pet.
pets.name: Pet name

housing.helper: Update your main housing costs. Other housing details from setup are preserved.

transport.helper: Update transport costs. Other vehicle details from setup are preserved.

health.helper: Update insurance premiums for each household member.

children.helper: Update cost amounts for each child. Add new costs from setup if needed.
children.noChildren: No children in your household profile.
children.noFields: No cost fields for this child.

settings.title: Settings
settings.revokeConsentConfirmMessage: This will delete all your data and progress. You will need to retake the questionnaire.
